The corrected version starts with "Seldom do," where "do" is the auxiliary verb. OR you can simply bring “the barriers between the races” before the adverb “seldom” as: The barriers between the races seldom seem less evident than on this language-leading team.So, the corrected sentence is: "Seldom do the barriers between the races seem less in evidence than on this language-leading team."
